LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANGUS CAMERON'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER OF DEFENDANT SHARON L. HAMILTON IS OVERRULED. NO USEFUL LITIGATION PURPOSE IS SERVED BY BRINGING THIS DEMURER, ESPECIALLY GIVEN THAT DEFENDANT IS A SELF-REPRESENTED LITIGANT, NOTWITHSTANDING THAT IT MAY HAVE SOME LEGAL MERIT AS TO SOME OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND THE RESULT, EVEN BY PLAINTIFF'S ACKNOWLEDGMENT, IS THAT DEFENDANT WILL BE GIVEN ANOTHER OPPORTUNITY TO FILE AN ANSWER. Mtn Compelling Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Mar-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William S. Weisberg, Esq., Weisber & Miller, 415-296-7070, for Plaintiff and moving party
Sharon L. Hamilton, 415-225-4498, defendant and opposing party in Pro Per

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
PLAINTIFF ANGUS CAMERON'S Motion Compelling Production Of Documents. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley as follows: DENIED. Plaintiff's November 15, 2011 subpoena violates the one deposition limit for natural persons does not apply because plaintiff's first subpoena was not necessary as preliminary discovery to ascertain the existence of documents or categories of documents after which a second deposition concerning the documents would be permissible. Requests for sanctions by both parties are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Off Calendar Nov-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM S. WEISBERG OF WEISBERG & MILLER, TEL. NO. 296-7070 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
SHARON HAMILTON, TEL. NO. 225-4498, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. GARY ANGEL, TEL. NO. 788-5935 APPEARING FOR DR. FRANCENE COLVIN.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF ANGUS CAMERON'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO SUBPOENA IS Denied. A valid proof of service was presented in open court that Plaintiff served the notice of the motion to compel on nonparty deponent Dr. Francene Colvin. (CCP 2025.480(c); Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1346.) The motion is denied as untimely. (CCP 2025.480(b).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller; Not reported. = (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ANGUS CAMERON'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER OF DEFENDANT SHARON L. HAMILTON.

APPEARANCES: Sharon Hamilton, 225-4498 deft & opposing party in propria persona; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's Demurrer to Answer is overruled. No useful litigation purpose is served by bringing this demurer, especially given that defendant is a self-represented litigant, notwithstanding that it may have some legal merit as to some of the affirmative defenses and the result, even by plaintiff's acknowledgment, is that defendant will be given another opportunity to file an answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
